monthly.headline: December concentrates $71B — 48% more than the monthly average
monthly.subline: The year-end "rush": Oct–Dec accumulate 30% of annual spend in just 3 months
monthly.ariaLabel: Monthly federal spending dot matrix, Jan to Dec 2023, each dot 1.6B MXN
monthly.footer: Source: COMPRANET 2023 · Each dot = 1.6B MXN · RUBLI v0.8.5

ghostNetwork.headline: 42 vendors, 5 addresses — the geography of invoicing fraud
ghostNetwork.lede: Constellation of definitive EFOS vendors sharing a handful of fiscal addresses. Each node is a vendor; size = contract count; color = risk score v0.8.5.
ghostNetwork.ariaLabel: Network of 42 ghost vendors clustered around 5 shared addresses
ghostNetwork.findingBody: Definitive EFOS vendors share a structural signature: few contracts (typically 2–5), short lifespan (2–3 years), and shared fiscal addresses. The v0.8.5 model underdetects them (score 0.28) because it was trained on large cases (IMSS, SEGALMEX) — a systematic detection gap.
ghostNetwork.footer: Source: SAT Art. 69-B CFF · cross-referenced with COMPRANET 2018–2024 · 38 RFC confirmed + 125 by name

emptyGranary.headline: Four companies, MXN $14.8B, zero competition
emptyGranary.subline: Top vendors by total value · AMLO 2019–24
emptyGranary.ariaLabel: Top agriculture vendors dot matrix, each dot 100M MXN
emptyGranary.findingBody: ILAS Mexico and Productos Loneg received 100% of their contracts without bidding. Average per contract: MXN 275M and MXN 302M respectively.
emptyGranary.footer: Source: COMPRANET · Each dot = $100M MXN · RUBLI v0.8.5 analysis

daBySector.headline: Each row is a sector — each dot is 2 percentage points
daBySector.subline: All cross the OECD line. Agriculture reaches 93% · Other, the lowest, triples the limit.
daBySector.ariaLabel: Direct award rate by sector as dot strip, 12 sectors, OECD ceiling at 25%

daRateTrend.headline: Direct award rose from 63% to 82% across three presidential terms
daRateTrend.subline: 3.3× the OECD limit of 25% — every administration worse than the last
daRateTrend.ariaLabel: Direct award rate by year 2010 to 2024, colored by presidential term

covidEmergency.headline: COVID-19 didn't create the crisis — it accelerated it
covidEmergency.subline: Direct award rose during the pandemic but never came back down. Single-bid hit 19.1% in 2021.

amloEra.headline: AMLO averaged 79.4% — every administration tops the last
amloEra.subline: +15.9 pts vs Fox era. The 81.9% peak is 3.3× the OECD limit.
amloEra.ariaLabel: DA peak rate by sexenio, dot matrix, each dot 1pp
amloEra.footer: Source: COMPRANET 2002–2025 · Each dot = 1pp · RUBLI v0.8.5
